meta.source: Microsoft Learn (learn.microsoft.com/exchange) — portal path, PowerShell parameters, and defaults grounded via the Microsoft Learn MCP on 2026-07-03. The other policies in this section share names with the Email Security playbook and inherit that guidance; this file covers the one Tier 1-only policy.
generalNotes: Reply-all storm protection is built into Exchange Online and on by default — the baseline values match Microsoft's platform defaults, so this is a verify-only item rather than something you build.

There's no configuration page in the Exchange admin center: the EAC only shows a read-only report (Reports > Mail flow > Reply-all storm protection). Any change is made in Exchange Online PowerShell with Set-TransportConfig.

requiredSettings.label: ReplyAllStormDetectionMinimumRecipients
requiredSettings.value: 2500 (a message must have at least this many recipients to be storm-eligible)

requiredSettings.label: ReplyAllStormBlockDurationHours
requiredSettings.value: 6 (further reply-alls to the thread are blocked for this long)

steps: Verify current settings without changing anything: in the new Exchange admin center, go to Reports > Mail flow > Reply-all storm protection — the top of the report shows Status, Minimum recipients, Minimum reply-alls, and Block duration hours (needs View-Only Configuration / Organization Transport Settings, included in Organization Management).

Or confirm in Exchange Online PowerShell: Get-TransportConfig | Select-Object ReplyAllStormProtectionEnabled, ReplyAllStormDetectionMinimumRecipients, ReplyAllStormDetectionMinimumReplies, ReplyAllStormBlockDurationHours

Only if a value has drifted from the baseline, correct it via PowerShell (there's no EAC config page), e.g.: Set-TransportConfig -ReplyAllStormProtectionEnabled $true -ReplyAllStormDetectionMinimumRecipients 2500 -ReplyAllStormDetectionMinimumReplies 10 -ReplyAllStormBlockDurationHours 6
Note: invisible to virtually all users — it only matters for very large distribution lists (2,500+ recipients). Changing settings mid-storm won't affect the current storm, only future ones.
